author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session centered on debugging a GitHub Actions workflow_dispatch error for the 360Chicago run and clearing up app package / OAuth registration confusion. The GitHub Actions API was repeatedly returning errors when listing workflow runs, making it impossible to check run status through the Teams agent interface. Investigation revealed that the repo was under the personal EMU account (Ameen-Hassan_ideas), not ideasorg, which placed it outside the enterprise runner provisioning scope. Additionally, clarified that the app had two registered OAuth apps and worked out which one to use. Session ended while debugging whether the app package needed to be repackaged to pick up the latest OAuth app reference.
problems: GitHub Actions API returning error for listWorkflowRuns: 'encountered an error when processing' — no run data retrievable through the Teams agent
GitHub-hosted runners not provisioned for repos under personal EMU account — only org-level repos get runner minutes
Two OAuth apps registered — unclear which one should be used in the app package
App package may be stale and need repackaging to pick up the correct OAuth app ID
approaches: Attempted to list workflow runs through GitHub Actions plugin — API errors persisted across retries
Provided raw repository API response to help diagnose run ID
Provided PAT and base64-encoded token values for authentication troubleshooting
Reviewed enterprise runner provisioning: standard runners had no status badge in the org, indicating runner minutes not provisioned
Investigated whether app needed to be repackaged vs just having secrets updated
results: Confirmed repo under personal EMU account is outside the enterprise runner provisioning scope
Standard GitHub-hosted runners showing no status badge in enterprise org — confirmed runner provisioning not set up
Two OAuth apps identified — need to reconcile which is active
Session ended while assessing repackaging need

decisions.decision: Repo must be under ideasorg to access GitHub-hosted runners provisioned at org level

open_questions: Which of the two registered OAuth apps is the correct one to use?
Does the app package need to be rebuilt and re-uploaded to pick up the new OAuth app ID?
How do we migrate the repo from personal EMU account to ideasorg?
